Reference_title: OsWRKY97, an Abiotic Stress-Induced Gene of Rice, Plays a Key Role in Drought Tolerance
Description: WRKY DNA binding domain containing protein, Positive regulation of drought tolerance

Reference_title: Multifaceted roles of zinc finger proteins in regulating various agronomic traits in rice
Description: Zinc finger CCCH domain-containing protein 35, Protein DELAY OF THE ONSET OF SENESCENCE-like, CCCH-tandem zinc finger protein, Photomorphogenesis and ABA responses, Abiotic stress tolerance

Gene name: Drought and Salt Tolerance gene, zinc finger transcription factor DST, DLN repressor 102, DLN motif protein 102, heat stress-tolerant 1
Reference_title: Multifaceted roles of zinc finger proteins in regulating various agronomic traits in rice.
Description: C2H2 zinc finger transcription factor, negatively regulating drought and salt tolerance, Stomatal aperture control, Control of leaf width

Reference_title: A Rice R2R3-Type MYB Transcription Factor OsFLP Positively Regulates Drought Stress Response via OsNAC
Description: MYB124, R2R3 MYB transcription factor, Regulation of the orientation of guard mother cell (GMC) symmetrical division, drought tolerance, Salt tolerance

Gene name: Low Cs accumulation, High-affinity Potassium(K+) Transporter 1, Potassium transporter 1
Reference_title: Molecular Mechanisms and Regulatory Pathways Underlying Drought Stress Response in Rice
Description: a drought-responsive gene that regulates K+ homeostasis to improve drought resistance in rice, Potassium transporter, Potassium mediated growth, Salt tolerance

Gene name: rice elongation factor 1A (eEFIA)-4, rice elongation factor 1A-4, ELONGATION FACTOR 1A, Elongation factor 1-alpha, EF-1-alpha, Eukaryotic elongation factor 1-alpha, elongation factor 1 alpha
Synonyms: RFA4, REFA4, refa4, eEF-1a, OsEF-1a, EF1A, eIF-1-alpha
Reference_title: Eukaryotic Translation Elongation Factor OsEF1A Positively Regulates Drought Tolerance and Yield in Rice
Description: Eukaryotic translation elongation factor, Positive regulation of drought tolerance, Positive regulation of tillering and yield

Gene name: MADS box gene23, MADS-box transcription factor 23
Reference_title: OsMADS23 phosphorylated by SAPK9 confers drought and salt tolerance by regulating ABA biosynthesis in rice
Description: MADS-box transcription factor 23, Regulation of ABA biosynthesis, Drought and salt tolerance, Osmotic stress tolerance

Reference_title: Rice Transcription Factor OsWRKY55 Is Involved in the Drought Response and Regulation of Plant Growth
Description: WRKY transcription factor, Component in the signal transduction pathways of auxin response, drought tolerance, Disease resistance

Reference_title: OsMLP423 Is a Positive Regulator of Tolerance to Drought and Salt Stresses in Rice
Description: Major latex protein, Bet v1 family protein, Positive regulation of drought and salinity tolerance

Synonyms: AWPM-19, PM19, PM1, OsPM-1, OsPM19L1, PM19L1, OsAWPM-19, OsPM19
Reference_title: OsPM1 is a positive regulator of rice tolerance to drought stress but a negative regulator of rice tolerance to salt stress
Description: AWPM-19-like protein, drought and stress tolerance through ABA-dependent pathway

Gene name: Calcineurin b-like protein-interacting protein kinase 23, CBL-interacting protein kinase 23, Sucrose nonfermenting-1-related protein kinase 3.23
Reference_title: Calcineurin B-like interacting protein kinase OsCIPK23 functions in pollination and drought stress responses in rice (Oryza sativa L.)
Description: Serine/threonine protein kinase, Pollination and drought stress (tolerance) responses, Reguration of potassium uptake by CBL1-CIPK23 complex

Reference: Xu, M., Zhang, M., Tu, Y., & Zhang, X. (2024).
Reference_title: Overexpression of the OsFes1A increased the phytosterols content and enhanced drought and salt stress tolerance in Arabidopsis
Description: The germination rate of transgenic Arabidopsis was significantly higher than WT under stress (salt, ABA, and drought treatment). In transgenic plants, the average content of campesterol was 17.6% higher than that of WT, and the average content of β-sitosterol reached 923.75 μg/g, with an increase of 1.33-fold.

Reference: Xie Z., Jin L., Sun Y., Zhan C., Tang S., Qin T., Liu N., Huang J. 2024
Reference_title: OsNAC120 balances plant growth and drought tolerance by integrating GA and ABA signaling in rice. Negative regulation of drought tolerance by repressing ABA-induced stomatal closure
Description: knockout of OsNAC120 inhibits plant growth but enhances drought tolerance, whereas OsNAC120 overexpression produces the opposite results.

Reference_title: OsFAR1 is involved in primary fatty alcohol biosynthesis and promotes drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsFAR1 was activated by abiotic stresses and abscisic acid, resulting in increased production of primary alcohol in rice. OsFAR1 overexpression lines exhibited reduced cuticle permeability and enhanced drought tolerance in rice and Arabidopsis.

Reference_title: OsOLP1 contributes to drought tolerance in rice by regulating ABA biosynthesis and lignin accumulation.
Description: Transgenic rice plants overexpressing OsOLP1 showed high drought tolerance with leaf water content of up to 65%, and a survival rate of 53.1% by regulating 96% stomatal closure and more than 2.5-fold proline content promotion through the accumulation of 1.5-fold endogenous ABA, and enhancing about 50% lignin synthesis.

Reference_title: Overexpression of OsHAD3, a Member of HAD Superfamily, Decreases drought tolerance of Rice.
Description: Under drought stress, overexpression of OsHAD3 increases the accumulation of reactive oxygen species and malondialdehyde than wild type. OsHAD3-overexpression lines decreased but antisense-expression lines increased the roots length under drought stress and the transcription levels of many well-known stress-related genes were also changed in plants with different genotypes.

Reference_title: Variations in OsSPL10 confer drought tolerance by directly regulating OsNAC2 expression and ROS production in rice.
Description: OsSPL10 appears to play a vital role in drought tolerance by controlling reactive oxygen species (ROS) production and stomatal movements. knockout of OsSPL10 induced fast stomatal closure and prevented water loss, thereby improving drought tolerance in rice.

Reference_title: The suppressed expression of a stress responsive gene 'OsDSR2' enhances rice tolerance in drought and salt stress.
Description: OsDSR2 knockdown exhibited improved salt and drought stress tolerance. OsDSR2 negatively affected rice's ability to withstand salt and drought.

Reference_title: OsWRKY76 positively regulates drought stress via OsbHLH148-mediated jasmonate signaling in rice.
Description: OsWRKY76 knockout weakened drought tolerance at the seedling stage and decreased MeJA sensitivity.

Synonyms: OsSPL14/WFP, Squamosa promoter-binding-like protein 14, Wealthy Farmer's Panicle
Reference_title: IPA1 improves drought tolerance by activating SNAC1 in rice.
Description: IPA1-knockout plants showed much greater accumulation of H2O2 as measured by 3,3'-diaminobenzidine staining in leaves compared with WT plants. IPA1 plays a positive role in drought-stress tolerance by inducing reactive oxygen species scavenging in rice.

Reference_title: Rice OsANN9 Enhances Drought Tolerance through Modulating ROS Scavenging Systems.
Description: Rice annexin protein, OsANN9, which in addition to regular annexin repeats and type-II Ca2+ binding sites, also consists of a C2H2-type zinc-finger domain. plants that overexpressed OsANN9 had increased survival rates under drought stress. In addition, the overexpression of OsANN9 increased superoxide dismutase (SOD), peroxidase (POD) and catalase (CAT) activities, which regulate reactive oxygen species homeostasis.

Reference_title: OsGSTU17, a Tau Class Glutathione S-Transferase Gene, Positively Regulates Drought Stress Tolerance in Oryza sativa.
Description: Under drought conditions, compared with the WT, the expressions of the drought stress-related genes OsNAC10, OsDREB2A, OsAP37, OsP5CS1, OsRAB16C, OsPOX1, OsCATA, and OsCATB in the osgtu17 mutant lines were significantly decreased. Knocking out OsGSTU17 significantly reduced the drought tolerance of rice.

Reference_title: Stromal ascorbate peroxidase (OsAPX7) modulates drought stress tolerance in rice (Oryza sativa).
Description: Under drought stress, silenced plants (APX7i) show a higher capacity to maintain stomata aperture and photosynthesis performance, resulting in a higher tolerance when compared to non-transformed plants. During drought stress, APX7i showed an up-regulation of genes encoding flavonoid and tyrosine metabolism enzymes and a down-regulation of genes related to phytohormones signal transduction and nicotinate and nicotinamide metabolism.

Synonyms: REK, rice endosperm protein kinase, OsSnRK2.3, Sucrose nonfermenting-1-related protein kinase 2.3
Reference_title: Molecular characterization reveals that OsSAPK3 improves drought tolerance and grain yield in rice.
Description: The expression of OsSAPK3 was up regulated by drought, NaCl, PEG and ABA. under drought conditions, mutants showed more intolerance to drought, including decreased survival rate, increased water loss rate, increased stomatal conductance and significantly decreased expression levels of SLAC1 and SLAC7. OsSAPK3 could improve rice yield by indirectly regulating tiller number and grain size.

Synonyms: OsCIPK24, OsSnRK3.22CBL-interacting protein kinase 24, low-cesium rice mutant 1, Sucrose nonfermenting-1-related protein kinase 3.22
Reference_title: Unraveling the contribution of OsSOS2 in conferring salinity and drought tolerance in a high‐yielding rice.
Description: OsSOS2, a SOS2 homolog from rice, improves salinity and drought tolerance.

Gene name: Suppressor of the G2 allele of skp1
Synonyms: subunit of the SCF ubiquitin ligase complex
Reference_title: Melatonin promotes SGT1-involved signals to ameliorate drought stress adaption in rice.
Description: OsSGT1 was involved in the drought response of rice and melatonin promoted SGT1-involved signals to ameliorate drought stress adaption.

Synonyms: OsC3H37, Zinc finger CCCH domain-containing protein 37
Reference_title: RNA binding protein OsTZF7 traffics between the nucleus and processing bodies/stress granules and positively regulates drought stress in rice.
Description: The expression of OsTZF7 was upregulated by abiotic stresses. The overexpression of OsTZF7 increased drought tolerance and enhanced sensitivity to exogenous ABA in rice.

Synonyms: OsPRI4, Positive Regulator Of Iron Homeostatis 4, transcription factor ILR3, OsMYC1
Reference_title: OsbHLH057 targets the AATCA cis-element to regulate disease resistance and drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Overexpressing OsbHLH057 enhanced rice disease resistance and drought tolerance, while knocking out OsbHLH057 made rice more susceptible to pathogens and drought.

Synonyms: OsLHY, OsLEM1,OsNhd1, OsDLN203, Late Elongated Hypocotyl, DLN repressor 203, DLN motif protein 203, lvp1 enhancer mutant 1, N-mediated heading date1
Reference_title: Rice CIRCADIAN CLOCK ASSOCIATED 1 transcriptionally regulates ABA signaling to confer multiple abiotic stress tolerance.
Description: OsCCA1 plays an essential role in conferring rice tolerance to multiple abiotic stresses, including salt, osmotic, and drought. oscca1 mutants also displayed hypersensitivity to drought stress, with two to three-fold reduction of survival rate compared to WT plants

Synonyms: SALT, OsJRL, OsMBL1, OsL43, Mannose-Binding Lectin 1, Jacalin-related lectin 1
Reference_title: OsSalT gene cloned from rice provides evidence of its role in salinity and drought stress tolerance.
Description: OsSalT transgenic tobacco plants showed increased tolerance to salinity and drought stress. OsSalT regulates the Reactive Oxygen Species (ROS) generating and scavenging system. OsSalT gene modulate ABA and GA signaling pathways

Reference_title: OsNAC016 regulates plant architecture and drought tolerance by interacting with the kinases GSK2 and SAPK8.
Description: Negative modulation of ABA-mediated drought tolerance, positive regulation of BR-regulated rice architecture.

Synonyms: OsIDD14, OsZOS1-06, indeterminate domain 14, zinc-finger protein TFIIIA class of Oryza sativa 1-06
Reference_title: Narrow lpa1 metaxylems enhance drought tolerance and optimize water use for grain filling in dwarf rice.
Description: Narrow metaxylem of lpa1 exhibit leaves curling on sunny day and convey drought tolerance but reduce grain yield in mature plants. Role of LPA1 in vasculature development, water transport, drought resistance, and grain yield.

Synonyms: OsERF25, AP2/EREBP138, OsDREB1I, ethylene response factor 25, APETALA2/ethylene-responsive element binding protein 138
Reference_title: Dehydration-responsive element binding protein 1C, 1E, and 1G promote stress tolerance to chilling, heat, drought, and salt in rice.
Description: OsDREB1G is a positive regulator of drought and salt tolerance.

Reference_title: Physio‐biochemical and molecular characterization of a rice drought‐insensitive TILLING line 1 (ditl1) mutant.
Description: The ditl1 mutant exhibited significantly decreased water loss, leaf curling, and H2 O2 accumulation under drought stress. Chlorophyll leaching assay and toluidine blue staining suggested lower cuticle permeability in ditl1 mutants than in wild-type (WT) plants. In addition, transmission electron microscopy revealed that ditl1 plants accumulated more cuticular wax on the epidermal surface.

Reference_title: OsGRP3 enhances drought resistance by altering phenylpropanoid biosynthesis pathway in rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: When compared with the WT, the OE lines developed better and had greater survival rates under drought stress conditions, whereas the KO lines showed lower survival rates.

Reference_title: Rice NAC17 transcription factor enhances drought tolerance by modulating lignin accumulation.
Description: Overexpressing OsNAC17 transgenic plants showed drought-tolerant, and knock-out plants exhibited drought susceptible phenotype compared to non-transgenic plants. OsNAC17 positively regulates several lignin biosynthetic genes and promotes lignin accumulation in leaves and roots.

Reference_title: A β-ketoacyl-CoA synthase OsCUT1 confers increased drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Compared to the wild type, overexpression of OsCUT1 exhibited significantly increased drought resistance. Epicuticular wax was increased on the leaf surface of overexpressed lines and the chlorophyll leaching experiment showed that the cuticular permeability was decreased in the overexpressed lines. Moreover, overexpression of OsCUT1 didn’t result in the significant changes of major agronomic traits.

Gene name: ABA Sensitivity-related leucine-rich repeat receptor-like kinase
Reference_title: Leucine-rich repeat receptor-like kinase OsASLRK regulates abscisic acid and drought responses via cooperation with S-like RNase OsRNS4 in rice.
Description: OsASLRK positively regulates drought tolerance and ABA sensitivity in rice. activities of SOD, POD, and CAT were increased and MDA (malondialdehyde) content and ion leakage were decreased in OsASLRK ectopic expression lines compared with those in the wild type. OsASLRK ectopic expression can enhance drought tolerance in rice, perhaps through the elevation of the activities of antioxidative enzymes and alleviating membrane damage.

Reference_title: OsWRKY114 negatively regulates drought tolerance by restricting stomatal closure in rice.
Description: The overexpression of OsWRKY114 significantly increased drought sensitivity in rice, which resulted in a lower survival rate after drought stress. Moreover, we showed that stomatal closure, which is a strategy to save water under drought, is restricted in OsWRKY114-overexpressing plants compared with wild-type plants.

Reference_title: Regulation of drought and salt tolerance by OsSKL2 and OsASR1 in rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsSKL2 in rice increased tolerance to salinity, drought and oxidative stress by increasing antioxidant enzyme activity, and reducing levels of H2O2, malondialdehyde, and relative electrolyte leakage. overexpression of OsSKL2 also increased sensitivity to exogenous ABA, with an increase in reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation.

Reference_title: Regulation of drought and salt tolerance by OsSKL2 and OsASR1 in rice.
Description: OsASR1, a well-known chaperone-like protein, exhibited positive roles in salt and drought tolerance. Under drought conditions, the WT plants exhibited more severe wilting and a yellowing phenotype than the OsASR1-OE lines.

Reference_title: OsFTL4, an FT-like gene, regulates flowering time and drought tolerance in rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: Silencing OsFTL4 enhances drought tolerance by decreasing stomatal conductance and water loss.

Reference_title: Rice OsPUB16 modulates the ‘SAPK9-OsMADS23-OsAOC’pathway to reduce plant water-deficit tolerance by repressing ABA and JA biosynthesis.
Description: OsPUB16, a U-box E3 ubiquitin ligase, negatively regulates rice drought response. Loss-of-function mutants of OsPUB16 generated through CRISPR/Cas9 system exhibited the markedly enhanced water-deficit tolerance, while OsPUB16 overexpression lines were hypersensitive to water deficit stress. Moreover, OsPUB16 negatively regulated ABA and JA response, and ospub16 mutants produced more endogenous ABA and JA than wild type when exposed to water deficit.

Gene name: Calcineurin B-like protein interacting protein kinase 17
Reference_title: Combined metabolomic and transcriptomic analysis reveals key components of OsCIPK17 overexpression improves drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsCIPK17 improved drought resistance of rice by regulating deep roots under drought stress. OsCIPK17 positively regulates rice drought resistance and participates in the accumulation of citric acid in the TCA cycle.

Reference_title: Overexpression of a DUF740 family gene (LOC_Os04g59420) imparts enhanced climate resilience through multiple stress tolerance in rice.
Description: Transgenic plants maintained higher RWC, photosynthetic pigments, and proline accumulation under drought and salinity stresses. Furthermore, they exhibited less accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) than PS2 under drought stress

Reference_title: Transcriptional activation of rice CINNAMOYL‐CoA REDUCTASE 10 by OsNAC5, contributes to drought tolerance by modulating lignin accumulation in roots.
Description: Transgenic rice plants overexpressing OsCCR10 showed improved drought tolerance at the vegetative stages of growth, as well as higher photosynthetic efficiency, lower water loss rates, and higher lignin content in roots compared to non-transgenic (NT) controls. OsCCr10 mediates drought tolerance through regulating lignin accumulation.

Reference_title: Transcriptional activation of rice CINNAMOYL‐CoA REDUCTASE 10 by OsNAC5, contributes to drought tolerance by modulating lignin accumulation in roots.
Description: Plants overexpressing OsNAC5 had significantly greater drought tolerance than control plants. OsNAC5 acts as an enhancer of OsCCR10 expression.

Synonyms: OsRPK3, OsMRLK63, OsMRLP15, receptor protein kinase 3, malectin/malectin-like receptor-like kinase 63, malectin/malectin-like receptor-like protein 15
Reference_title: Rice carbohydrate-binding malectin-like protein, OsCBM1, contributes to drought-stress tolerance by participating in NADPH oxidase-mediated ROS production.
Description: Overexpression of OsCBM1 increased drought tolerance and reactive oxygen species production in rice, whereas the knockdown of the gene decreased them. OsCBM1 physically interacts with OsRbohA, a NADPH oxidase, and the expression of OsCBM1 in osrbohA, an OsRbohA-knockout mutant, is significantly downregulated under both normal growth and drought stress conditions.

Reference_title: miR2105 and the kinase OsSAPK10 co-regulate OsbZIP86 to mediate drought-induced ABA biosynthesis in rice.
Description: OsSAPK10 phosphorylates OsbZIP86 to positively regulate OsNCED3 expression. miR2105-(OsSAPK10)-OsbZIP86-OsNCED3" module regulates the drought-induced ABA biosynthesis without penalty on rice growth under normal conditions

Reference_title: miR2105 and the kinase OsSAPK10 co-regulate OsbZIP86 to mediate drought-induced ABA biosynthesis in rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsbZIP86 and dow.nregulation of miR2105 improve drought tolerance without affecting the main agronomic traits of rice

Reference_title: OsSCL30 overexpression reduces the tolerance of rice seedlings to low temperature, drought and salt.
Description: Overexpression of OsSCL30 diminished the resistance to low temperature, drought and salt stresses in transgenic rice and resulted in larger accumulation of reactive oxygen species.

Reference_title: OsHsfB4b confers enhanced drought tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis and rice.
Description: Over-expression of the OsHsfB4b gene in Arabidopsis and rice can increase the resistance to drought stress.

Synonyms: OsNAC1, OsDLN172, OsY37, ONAC11, OMTN4, Oryza miR164-targeted NAC4, Yellow37
Reference_title: Knocking out the transcription factor OsNAC092 promoted rice drought tolerance.
Description: OsNAC092 mutant rice exhibited a higher ability to scavenge more ROS and maintained a high GSH/GSSG ratio and redox level under drought stress, which could protect cells from oxidant stress.

Reference_title: Ubiquitin ligase OsRINGzf1 regulates drought resistance by controlling the turnover of OsPIP2; 1.
Description: The OsRINGzf1 OE plants, with higher leaf-related water content (LRWC) and lower leaf water loss rate (LWLR), exhibited enhanced drought resistance, whereas the RNAi and knockout plants of OsRINGzf1 were more sensitive to drought. OsRINGzf1 positively regulates drought resistance through promoting the degradation of OsPIP2;1 to enhance water retention capacity in rice.

Reference_title: Ubiquitin ligase OsRINGzf1 regulates drought resistance by controlling the turnover of OsPIP2; 1.
Description: The interaction between OsRINGzf1 and OsPIP2;1 reduces the water loss under drought stress

Synonyms: OsPTK11, Protein Tyrosine Kinase 11, probable serine/threonine-protein kinase PBL2
Reference_title: Overexpression of OsRLCK241 confers enhanced salt and drought tolerance in transgenic rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: OsRLCK241 expression improved ROS detoxification by enhancing the activities of ROS scavengers as well as the accumulation of compatible osmolytes to alleviate the osmotic stress evoked by salt and drought stresses.

Reference_title: The rice aldehyde oxidase OsAO3 gene regulates plant growth, grain yield, and drought tolerance by participating in ABA biosynthesis.
Description: Overexpression of OsAO3 increased ABA levels, increased drought tolerance, decreased seedling growth and decreased grain yield.

Reference_title: OsCRP1, a ribonucleoprotein gene, regulates chloroplast mRNA stability that confers drought and cold tolerance.
Description: OsCRP1 overexpressing plants showed a higher cyclic electron transport (CET) activity, which is essential for elevated levels of ATP for photosynthesis. Overexpression of OsCRP1 resulted in significantly enhanced drought and cold stress tolerance with higher ATP levels compared to wild type.

Synonyms: OsPYL/RCAR4, Regulatory components of ABA receptor 4
Reference: Santosh Kumar, V. V., et al. 2021
Reference_title: The abscisic acid receptor OsPYL6 confers drought tolerance to indica rice through dehydration avoidance and tolerance mechanisms.
Description: OsPYL6 overexpression enhanced the expression of stress-responsive genes and dehydration tolerance. Overexpression of OsPYL6 conferred ABA hypersensitivity during germination and promoted total root length. However, overexpression of PYL6 reduced grain yield under non-stress conditions due to reduction in height, biomass, panicle branching and spikelet fertility.

Reference_title: OsIAA20, an Aux/IAA protein, mediates abiotic stress tolerance in rice through an ABA pathway.
Description: OsIAA20 overexpression plants increased drought and salt tolerance. Overexpressing OsIAA20 exhibited increased stomatal closure and decreased the rate of water loss.

Reference_title: Nuclear translocation of OsMFT1 that is impeded by OsFTIP1 promotes drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsMFT1 might act as a hitherto unknown nucleocytoplasmic trafficking signal that regulates drought tolerance in rice in response to environmental signals.

Reference_title: Nuclear translocation of OsMFT1 that is impeded by OsFTIP1 promotes drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsFTIP1 negatively regulates drought response in rice. OsFTIP1 interacts with OsMFT1, a PEBP that promotes rice tolerance to drought treatment.

Synonyms: OsUPS, OsCMPG1, U-box containing E3 ligase induced by phosphate starvation, immediate-early fungal elicitor protein CMPG1
Reference_title: OsPUB41, a U-box E3 ubiquitin ligase, acts as a negative regulator of drought stress response in rice (Oryza Sativa L.).
Description: OsPUB41 plays a negative role in drought stress response through the mediation of OsUBC25 and interacts with OsCLC6, suggesting a putative substrate.

Synonyms: qRA2, Cytokinin-O-glucosyltransferase2, QTL for ratooning ability on chromosome 2, 7-deoxyloganetin glucosyltransferase
Reference_title: Overexpression of OsUGT3 enhances drought and salt tolerance through modulating ABA synthesis and scavenging ROS in rice.
Description: Overexpression of UGT3 in rice increased endogenous ABA level and showed hypersensitive phenotype to exogenous ABA treatment at both germination and post-germination stages. The ectopic expression of UGT3 in Arabidopsis also enhanced the plant tolerance to drought and salt stresses.

Reference_title: H3K36 methyltransferase SDG708 enhances drought tolerance by promoting abscisic acid biosynthesis in rice.
Description: SDG708, a specific H3K36 methyltransferase, functions as a positive regulator of drought tolerance in rice. SDG708 induced hydrogen peroxide accumulation in the guard cells and promoted stomatal closure to reduce water loss. Overexpression of SDG708 concomitantly enhanced rice drought tolerance and increased grain yield under normal and drought stress conditions.

Reference: Singh, Ajit Pal, Balaji Mani, and Jitender Giri. 2021
Reference_title: OsJAZ9 is involved in water-deficit stress tolerance by regulating leaf width and stomatal density in rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsJAZ9 imparts water deficit (drought) tolerance in rice. Overexpression of OsJAZ9 led to the enhanced ABA and JA levels. Further, OsJAZ9 overexpression reduces leaf width and stomata density, leading to lower leaf transpiration rates than WT. This reduced transpiration and higher K content as osmoticum improved the water-deficit stress tolerance in OsJAZ9 overexpression lines.

Reference_title: OsPSKR15, a phytosulfokine receptor from rice enhances abscisic acid response and drought stress tolerance.
Description: OsPSKR15 is a positive regulator in plant response to ABA. Ectopic expression of OsPSKR15 in Arabidopsis thaliana increased the sensitivity to ABA during germination, growth and stomatal closure. Constitutive expression of OsPSKR15 enhanced drought tolerance by reducing the transpirational water loss in Arabidopsis.

Reference_title: Overexpression of Rice Os S1Fa1 Gene Confers Drought Tolerance in Arabidopsis.
Description: OsS1Fa1 overexpression in Arabidopsis induced the expression of selected drought-responsive genes and enhanced the survival rate of transgenic lines under drought.

Synonyms: OsTZF8, OsCCCH-Zn-2, OsEnS-12, Tandem zinc finger protein 8, endosperm-specific gene 12
Reference_title: Overexpression of OsC3H10, a CCCH-Zinc Finger, Improves Drought Tolerance in Rice by Regulating Stress-Related Genes.
Description: Root-specific overexpression of OsC3H10 was insufficient to induce drought tolerance, while the overexpression of OsC3H10 throughout the entire plant enhanced the drought tolerance of rice plants.

Synonyms: Cen-like protein FDR2, Putative phosphatidylethanolamine-binding protein
Reference_title: RICE CENTRORADIALIS 1, a TFL1-like Gene, Responses to Drought Stress and Regulates Rice Flowering Transition.
Description: Drought can interfere with flowering; therefore, many plants hasten this process to shorten their life cycle under water scarcity, and this is known as drought-escape response. However, rice has other strategies; for example, drought stress can delay flowering instead of accelerating it. RCN1 plays an important role in the process of drought stress inhibiting flowering transition. It may worked by suppressing the protein function rather than transcription of HEADING DATE 3a.

Reference_title: OsRbohB-mediated ROS production plays a crucial role in drought stress tolerance of rice.
Description: Rice NADPH oxidase gene OsRbohB contributes drought tolerance and its functions are involved in the interaction of the OsRbohB-mediated ROS production and ABA signaling.

Synonyms: ONAC16, SNAC3, ONAC066, stress-responsive NAC transcription factor 3, SECONDARY WALL-ASSOCIATED NAC PROTEIN3
Reference_title: Knockout of the OsNAC006 Transcription Factor Causes Drought and Heat Sensitivity in Rice.
Description: RNA sequencing (RNA-seq) transcriptome analysis revealed that OsNAC006 regulates the expression of genes mainly involved in response to stimuli, oxidoreductase activity, cofactor binding, and membrane-related pathways. Knockout of OsNAC006 using the CRISPR-Cas9 system resulted in drought and heat sensitivity.

Reference: El-Esawi, Mohamed A., and Aisha A. Alayafi. 2019)
Reference_title: Overexpression of rice Rab7 gene improves drought and heat tolerance and increases grain yield in rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: OsRab7 gene improves grain yield and enhances drought and heat tolerance in transgenic rice by modulating osmolytes, antioxidants and abiotic stress-responsive genes expression.

Reference: Kang, Zhenhui, Tong Qin, and Zhiping Zhao. 2019
Reference_title: Overexpression of the zinc finger protein gene OsZFP350 improves root development by increasing resistance to abiotic stress in rice.
Description: Expression of OsZFP350 was up-regulated by salt, drought and high temperature, indicating that it might be a regulator in response to abiotic stress in rice root.  OsZFP350 plays a positive role in the adaptability of rice roots to abiotic stress.

Reference: Piao, Weilan, Yasuhito Sakuraba, and Nam-Chon Paek. 2019
Reference_title: Transgenic expression of rice MYB102 (OsMYB102) delays leaf senescence and decreases abiotic stress tolerance in Arabidopsis thaliana.
Description: The OsMYB102-OX plants showed a delayed senescence phenotype during dark incubation and were more susceptible to salt and drought stresses, considerably similar to Arabidopsis plants overexpressing AtMYB44.

Synonyms: SAP and MIZ 1, OsDLN131, OsPHD23, DLN motif protein 131, Plant homeo-domain finger protein 23
Reference_title: Co-overexpression of AVP1 and OsSIZ1 in Arabidopsis substantially enhances plant tolerance to drought, salt, and heat stresses.
Description: Overexpression of the rice SUMO E3 ligase gene OsSIZ1 improves heat and water deficit stress tolerance in transgenic plants. AVP1/OsSIZ1 co-overexpressing plants performed significantly better than AVP1-overexpressing plants and OsSIZ1-overexpressing plants, and produced 100% more seed than wild-type plants under single stress or multiple stress conditions.

Synonyms: OsTIFY3, OsJAZ10, EG2, extra glume 2, TIFY gene 3
Reference_title: OsJAZ1 attenuates drought resistance by regulating JA and ABA signaling in rice.
Description: Expression of OsJAZ1 was strongly responsive to JA treatment, and it was slightly responsive to ABA, salicylic acid, and abiotic stresses including drought, salinity, and cold. The OsJAZ1-overexpression rice plants were more sensitive to drought stress treatment than the wild-type (WT) rice Zhonghua 11 (ZH11) at both the seedling and reproductive stages, while the jaz1 T-DNA insertion mutant plants showed increased drought tolerance compared to the WT plants.

Synonyms: AP2/EREBP170, OsDRAP1, APETALA2/ethylene-responsive element binding protein 170, Drought Responsive AP2/EREBP gene 1, ethylene-responsive transcription factor RAP2-4
Reference_title: Overexpression of OsERF 48 causes regulation of Os CML 16, a calmodulin‐like protein gene that enhances root growth and drought tolerance.
Description: OsERF48 is a drought-inducible transcriptional activator. Overexpression of OsERF48 in rice confers drought tolerance at the vegetative stage.

Reference_title: Overexpression of OsERF 48 causes regulation of Os CML 16, a calmodulin‐like protein gene that enhances root growth and drought tolerance.
Description: OsCML16, a calmodulin-like protein gene enhances root growth and drought tolerance.

Reference_title: OsASR 5 enhances drought tolerance through a stomatal closure pathway associated with ABA and H2O2 signalling in rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsASR5 enhanced osmotic tolerance in Escherichia coli and drought tolerance in Arabidopsis and rice by regulating leaf water status under drought stress conditions. Moreover, overexpression of OsASR5 in rice increased endogenous ABA level and showed hypersensitive to exogenous ABA treatment at both germination and postgermination stages.

Reference_title: The rice Os NAC 6 transcription factor orchestrates multiple molecular mechanisms involving root structural adaptions and nicotianamine biosynthesis for drought tolerance.
Description: OsNAC6 root-specific overexpressing transgenic rice lines was less affected by drought stress than were nontransgenic controls. OsNAC6 up-regulates the expression of direct target genes involved in membrane modification, nicotianamine (NA) biosynthesis, glutathione relocation, 3'-phophoadenosine 5'-phosphosulphate accumulation and glycosylation, which represent multiple drought tolerance pathways.

Reference_title: Overexpression of OsDT11, which encodes a novel cysteine-rich peptide, enhances drought tolerance and increases ABA concentration in rice.
Description: OsDT11-overexpression lines displayed dramatically enhanced tolerance to drought and had reduced water loss, reduced stomatal density, and an increased the concentration of abscisic acid (ABA). The suppression of OsDT11 expression resulted in an increased sensitivity to drought compared to wild-type expression.

Gene name: salt-sensitive K1 uptake channel OsAKT1 K+ channel alpha subunit
Reference: Ahmad, Izhar, Afaq Mian, and Frans JM Maathuis. 2016
Reference_title: Overexpression of the rice AKT1 potassium channel affects potassium nutrition and rice drought tolerance.
Description: AKT1 overexpression improved rice osmotic and drought stress tolerance by increasing tissue levels of K(+), especially in the root.

Reference_title: OsMSR15 encoding a rice C2H2-type zinc finger protein confers enhanced drought tolerance in transgenic Arabidopsis.
Description: Expression of OsMSR15 in Arabidopsis conferred drought tolerance, and transgenic plants showed hypersensitivity to exogenous ABA during the seed germination and post-germination stages. Transgenic plants also showed higher levels of free proline, less electrolyte leakage and increased expressions of a number of stress-responsive genes, including LEA3, RD29A, DREB1A and P5CS1 under drought stress.

Synonyms: OsPYL/RCAR9, OsPYL2, PYRABACTIN RESISTANCE 1 LIKE/REGULATORY COMPONENTS OF ABA RECEPTOR 9
Reference_title: ABA receptor PYL9 promotes drought resistance and leaf senescence.
Description: PYL9 transgenic lines showed dramatically increased drought resistance and drought-induced leaf senescence in both Arabidopsis and rice.

Synonyms: ERF117, AP2/EREBP63, APETALA2/ethylene-responsive element binding protein 63
Reference_title: The ethylene response factor OsERF109 negatively affects ethylene biosynthesis and drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Transgenic plants overexpressing (OE) and knockdown with RNA interfering (RI) OsERF109 exhibited significantly reduced and improved drought resistance, respectively, indicating that OsERF109 negatively regulates drought resistance in rice.

Reference: Chen J, Zhang J, Fang C, Ren L, Lan T, Wu W and Lan T (2024).
Reference_title: OsPDIL1-5: dual role in promoting growth and development while modulating drought stress tolerance in rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: OsPDIL1-5 acts as a negative regulator of drought tolerance. The loss of OsPDIL1-5 function inhibits plant growth, reduces stomatal density, size and aperture, boosts ROS scavenging system activity, and ultimately enhances drought tolerance.

Reference_title: Over‐expression of a single Ca2+‐dependent protein kinase confers both cold and salt/drought tolerance on rice plants.
Description: Over-expression of OsCDPK7 enhanced induction of some stress-responsive genes in response to salinity/drought.

Reference_title: A rice calcium-dependent protein kinase OsCPK9 positively regulates drought stress tolerance and spikelet fertility.
Description: The results of OsCPK9 overexpression (OsCPK9-OX) and OsCPK9 RNA interference (OsCPK9-RNAi) analyses revealed that OsCPK9 plays a positive role in drought stress tolerance and spikelet fertility. OsCPK9 plays a positive role in drought, osmotic, and dehydration stress responses. OsCPK9 confers tolerance to drought stress by improving osmotic adjustment and stomatal movement.

Synonyms: OsMYB48-2, OsMYB59, Os2R_MYB19, R2R3-MYB Transcription Factor 19
Reference_title: Overexpression of OsMYB48-1, a novel MYB-related transcription factor, enhances drought and salinity tolerance in rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsMYB48-1 could regulate the expression of some ABA biosynthesis genes (OsNCED4, OsNCED5), early signaling genes (OsPP2C68, OSRK1) and late responsive genes (RAB21, OsLEA3, RAB16C and RAB16D) under drought stress conditions.  the overexpression lines exhibited reduced rate of water loss, lower malondialdehyde (MDA) content and higher proline content under stress conditions. Moreover, overexpression plants were hypersensitive to ABA at both germination and post-germination stages and accumulated more endogenous ABA under drought stress conditions.

Reference_title: Rice OsGL1-6 is involved in leaf cuticular wax accumulation and drought resistance.
Description: OsGL1-6 antisense-RNA transgenic plants showed droopy leaves at the booting stage, significantly decreased leaf cuticular wax deposition, thinner cuticle membrane, increased chlorophyll leaching and water loss rates, and enhanced drought sensitivity.

Reference_title: Characterization of Glossy1-homologous genes in rice involved in leaf wax accumulation and drought resistance.
Description: OsGL1-2 overexpression resulted in increased amounts of cuticular waxes and enhanced drought tolerance

Reference_title: Calcium-dependent protein kinase OsCPK10 mediates both drought tolerance and blast disease resistance in rice plants.
Description: OsCPK10 protein enhances the hydrogen peroxide detoxifying capacity of rice plants during desiccation. OsCPK10HA expression improves drought tolerance in rice plants.

Reference_title: OsBBP1, a newly identified protein containing DUF630 and DUF632 domains confers drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsBBP1 might confer drought resistance by inducing more efficient reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging. OsBBP1, acting downstream of OsbZIP72, contributes to drought resistance and causes less yield or yield-related losses under drought conditions.

Reference_title: OsJAZ10 negatively modulates the drought tolerance by integrating hormone signaling with systemic electrical activity in rice.
Description: OsJAZ10 negatively modulates rice drought stress tolerance. OsJAZ10 interacts with OsMYC2, and inhibits OsMYC2 transcriptional activation of ABA- and JA-biosynthetic genes. OsJAZ10 inhibits osmotic stress-elicited electrical signal transmission, which is in parallel to the hormone response.

Reference_title: OsJAZ10 negatively modulates the drought tolerance by integrating hormone signaling with systemic electrical activity in rice.
Description: OsMYC2 transcriptionally activates the expression of ABA-biosynthetic gene OsNCED2, JA-biosynthetic gene OsAOC, and drought-responsive genes OsRAB21 and OsLEA3, while OsJAZ10 prevents OsMYC2 transactivation of these genes.

Synonyms: OsLEA21, late embryogenesis abundant protein 21, ABA regulated gene
Reference_title: Overexpression of OsEm1 encoding a group I LEA protein confers enhanced drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsEm1 is a positive regulator of drought tolerance. Overexpression of OsEm1 increases ABA sensitivity and enhances osmotic tolerance in rice. Compared with wild type, the OsEm1-overexpressing rice plants showed enhanced plant survival ratio at the vegetative stage. Overexpression of OsEm1 increases the expression of LEA genes against drought.

Reference_title: Overexpression of a Stress-Responsive NAC Transcription Factor Gene ONAC022 Improves Drought and Salt Tolerance in Rice.
Description: ONAC022 functions as a stress-responsive NAC with transcriptional activator activity and plays a positive role in drought and salt stress tolerance through modulating an ABA-mediated pathway.

Reference_title: OsWRKY12 negatively regulates the drought-stress tolerance and secondary cell wall biosynthesis by targeting different downstream transcription factor genes in rice.
Description: Over-expression of OsWRKY12 decreased the drought tolerance of the rice transgenics at the germination stage and the seedling stage. OsWRKY12 negatively regulates the drought-stress response by affecting the ABA biosynthesis and signaling pathway. Rice seedlings over-expressing OsWRKY12 showed decreased secondary cell wall (SCW) biosynthesis. OsWRKY12 mediates the crosstalk of the drought-stress response and SCW biosynthesis by targeting to different transcription factor genes.

Reference_title: Rice WRKY11 Plays a Role in Pathogen Defense and Drought Tolerance.
Description: OsWRKY11 positively regulates drought-responsive genes, including several dehydrin genes. OsWRKY11 is a positive regulator of plant defense responses to drought and pathogens. OsWRKY11 is induced by heat and drought stresses, and over-expression of OsWRKY11 under the control of the HSP101 promoter enhances drought tolerance. OsWRKY11 also bound directly to the promoter of a drought-responsive gene, RAB21, activating its transcription.

Reference_title: Overexpression of rice glutaredoxin genes LOC_Os02g40500 and LOC_Os01g27140 regulate plant responses to drought stress.
Description: Over-expression of Osgrx genes enhanced drought tolerance in Arabidopsis. The H2O2 level was found to be reduced in all transgenic lines of both Osgrxs LOC_Os02g40500 and LOC_Os01g27140 as compared to treated control plants. Over-expression of Osgrxs enhanced plant growth during drought conditions. Drought tolerance was found in transgenic plantlets of both Osgrx genes after 10 days of drought as compared to retarded growth in treated control plants. Osgrxs enhanced the RWC and reduced the stress markers. Each glutaredoxin gene was over-expressed in Arabidopsis thaliana to reveal their role in drought stress.

Reference_title: Overexpression of rice glutaredoxin genes LOC_Os02g40500 and LOC_Os01g27140 regulate plant responses to drought stress.
Description: LOC_Os02g40500 and LOC_Os01g27140 reduced the ROS generated under drought stress. Osgrxs enhanced the level of the antioxidant molecules, antioxidant enzymes, and stress responsible amino acids. Osgrxs enhanced the various physiological parameters against drought stress and protected the plant from damage. Each glutaredoxin gene was over-expressed in Arabidopsis thaliana to reveal their role in drought stress.

Synonyms: Cysteine-rich and transmembrane domain-containing protein WIH
Reference_title: The bHLH transcription factor regulated gene OsWIH2 is a positive regulator of drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsWIH2 in rice resulted in significantly higher drought tolerance, due to the decreased water loss rate and reactive oxygen species (ROS) accumulation under drought stress. Drought stress may induce OsbHLH130 accumulation, which in turn activates OsWIH2 expression, and the latter improves rice drought tolerance by participating in cuticular wax biosynthesis and reducing the water loss rate as well as ROS accumulation. OsWIH2 interacted with a long-chain fatty acids HOTHEAD (HTH).

Reference_title: OsMT1a, a type 1 metallothionein, plays the pivotal role in zinc homeostasis and drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Transgenic rice plants overexpressing OsMT1a demonstrated enhanced tolerance to drought. The examination of antioxidant enzyme activities demonstrated that catalase (CAT), peroxidase (POD) and ascorbate peroxidase (APX) were significantly elevated in transgenic plants.

Reference_title: Involvement of the Metallothionein gene OsMT2b in Drought and Cadmium Ions Stress in Rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsMT2b positively regulates drought tolerance in rice. OsMT2b-overexpression lines exhibited enhanced drought tolerance and better physiological performance in terms of relative water content and electrolyte leakage compared with wild-type (WT).  OsMT2b influenced the expression of genes involved in ROS scavenging to enhance drought tolerance in rice.

Reference_title: OsADR3 increases drought stress tolerance by inducing antioxidant defense mechanisms and regulating OsGPX1 in rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: Overexpression of abscisic acid (ABA)-drought-reactive oxygen species 3 (OsADR3) in rice enhances sensitivity to exogenous ABA and increases the activities of various antioxidant enzymes, including catalase (CAT), glutathione peroxidase, and ascorbate peroxidase (APX), thus improving ROS scavenging capacity to positively regulate drought tolerance

Reference_title: OsMIOX, a myo-inositol oxygenase gene, improves drought tolerance through scavenging of reactive oxygen species in rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: OsMIOX was induced by drought, H2O2, salt, cold and abscisic acid. OsMIOX has a specific function in drought stress tolerance by decreasing oxidative damage. Overexpression of OsMIOX increased the ROS-scavenging ability in rice. Overexpression of OsMIOX improves rice tolerance to drought stress.

Reference_title: Rice (Oryza sativa L.) tau class glutathione S-transferase (OsGSTU30) overexpression in Arabidopsis thaliana modulates a regulatory network leading to heavy metal and drought stress tolerance.
Description: Overexpression of OsGSTU30 increased drought tolerance, as reflected in germination, root growth assay, and whole plant growth.

Reference_title: Over-expression of oshox4 enhances drought and salinity tolerance in rice.
Description: Oshox4 transgenic rice plants are more tolerant to drought and salinity stress and that Oshox4 plays a role in rice osmatic tolerance.

Synonyms: OsRING161, RING-type E3 ubiquitin ligase 161, E3 ubiquitin-protein ligase RZFP34
Reference_title: DCA1 Acts as a Transcriptional Co-activator of DST and Contributes to Drought and Salt Tolerance in Rice.
Description: Downregulation of DCA1 significantly enhanced drought and salt tolerance in rice, and overexpression of DCA1 increased sensitivity to stress treatment. Our findings establish a framework for plant drought and salt stress tolerance through the DCA1-DST (Drought and salt tolerance)-Prx24 (Peroxidase 24) pathway.

Reference_title: Overexpression of a TFIIIA-type zinc finger protein gene ZFP252 enhances drought and salt tolerance in rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: Overexpression of ZFP252 in rice increased the amount of free proline and soluble sugars, elevated the expression of stress defense genes and enhanced rice tolerance to salt and drought stresses, as compared with ZFP252 antisense and non-transgenic plants.

Reference_title: OsWRKY45 alleles play different roles in abscisic acid signalling and salt stress tolerance but similar roles in drought and cold tolerance in rice.
Description: Overexpression of a stress-induced OsWRKY45 significantly confers drought tolerance in Arabidopsis and rice.

Synonyms: GEM-like protein 7, Glucosyltransferases-like GTPase activators and Myotubularin (GRAM) domain-containing protein
Reference_title: OsABAR1, a novel GRAM domain-containing protein, confers drought and salt tolerance via an ABA-dependent pathway in rice.
Description: OsABAR1 expression is up-regulated by salt and drought. OsABAR1 positively regulates tolerance to drought and high salinity in rice. OsABAR1 acts as a positive regulator of ABA responses in rice.

Reference_title: Identification of OsbZIP72 as a positive regulator of ABA response and drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Transgenic rice overexpressing OsbZIP72 showed a hypersensitivity to ABA, elevated levels of expression of ABA response gene such as LEAs, and an enhanced ability of drought tolerance.

Reference_title: Overexpression of a novel MYB-related transcription factor, OsMYBR1, confers improved drought tolerance and decreased ABA sensitivity in rice.
Description: OsMYBR1 is involved in the ABA-dependent signaling transduction pathways mediating plant responses to ABA and drought. Compared with wild-type (WT) plants, transgenic plants over-expressing OsMYBR1 exhibited much greater tolerance to drought stress and decreased sensitivity to abscisic acid (ABA). Overexpression of OsMYBR1 enhances tolerance to dehydration stress

Reference_title: A R2R3-type MYB gene, OsMYB2, is involved in salt, cold, and dehydration tolerance in rice.
Description: OsMYB2-overexpressing plants were more tolerant to salt, cold, and dehydration stresses and more sensitive to abscisic acid than wild-type plants. verexpression of OsMYB2 enhanced up-regulation of genes encoding proline synthase and transporters. The OsMYB2-overexpressing plants accumulated less amounts of H2O2 and malondialdehyde.

Reference_title: The NAC family transcription factor OsNAP confers abiotic stress response through the ABA pathway.
Description: Rice plants overexpressing OsNAP did not show growth retardation, but showed a significantly reduced rate of water loss, enhanced tolerance to high salinity, drought and low temperature at the vegetative stage, and improved yield under drought stress at the flowering stage. plants overexpressing OsNAP revealed that many stress-related genes were up-regulated, including OsPP2C06/OsABI2, OsPP2C09, OsPP2C68 and OsSalT.

Reference_title: Root-specific expression of OsNAC10 improves drought tolerance and grain yield in rice under field drought conditions.
Description: Overexpression of OsNAC10 enhanced the tolerance of rice to drought stress at the vegetative stage, and specific overexpression of OsNAC10 in rice roots increased yields under normal and drought conditions

Reference_title: OsWRKY30 is activated by MAP kinases to confer drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsWRKY30 in rice dramatically increased drought tolerance. OsWRKY30 could be phosphorylated by OsMPK3/7/14 which was indispensable for the improved drought tolerance of the OsWRKY30- overexpressors

Reference_title: The rice transcription factor OsWRKY47 is a positive regulator of the response to water deficit stress.
Description: OsWRKY47 expression is induced in water stressed plants. OsWRKY47 overexpressors display higher yield than controls under drought stress.

Synonyms: OsABF7, OsDLN149, HBF1, Hd3a BINDING REPRESSOR FACTOR1
Reference_title: OsbZIP42 is a positive regulator of ABA signaling and confers drought tolerance to rice.
Description: Transgenic rice overexpressing OsbZIP42 (OsbZIP42-OX) exhibited a rapidly elevated expression of the ABA-responsive LEA3 and Rab16 genes and was hypersensitive to ABA. Analyses of the OsbZIP42-OX plants revealed enhanced tolerance to drought stress.

Synonyms: OsABF5, OsTRAB1, transcription factor responsible for ABA regulation 1
Reference_title: Overexpression of the OsbZIP66 transcription factor enhances drought tolerance of rice plants.
Description: Overexpression of OsbZIP66 both in a whole plant body and specifically in roots enhanced drought tolerance of rice plants, indicating that the rice drought tolerance positively correlates with the expression levels of OsbZIP66.

Reference: Lee, Y. H., & Song, S. I. (2023)
Reference_title: OsbZIP62 positively regulates drought and salt stress tolerance and ABA signaling in rice.
Description: The overexpression of rice OsbZIP62 gene increased the drought stress tolerance in rice. OsbZIP62 can interact with Stress-/ABA-activated protein kinases1, 2, 4, and 6, which have been known to phosphorylate OsbZIPs in rice. The transgenic rice overexpressed the OsbZIP62 gene exhibited an increase of drought and high-salinity stress tolerance.

Reference_title: Inactivating transcription factor OsWRKY5 enhances drought tolerance through abscisic acid signaling pathways.
Description: OsWRKY5 negatively regulates drought tolerance by directly binding to the promoter of OsMYB2 and hereby inhibiting its expression

Reference_title: OsIAA18, an Aux/IAA Transcription Factor Gene, Is Involved in Salt and Drought Tolerance in Rice.
Description: OsIAA18 overexpression in rice plants significantly improves salt and drought tolerance by upregulating ABA-regulated gene expression levels and improving ROS scavenging capacity.

Reference_title: OsIAA6, a member of the rice Aux/IAA gene family, is involved in drought tolerance and tiller outgrowth.
Description: OsIAA6 overexpressed transgenic rice improved drought tolerance, likely via the regulation of auxin biosynthesis genes. OsIAA6 is highly induced by drought stress. OsIAA6 was specifically expressed in the axillary meristem of the basal stem, which is the tissue that gives rise to tillers.

Reference_title: Overexpression of a zinc-finger protein gene from rice confers tolerance to cold, dehydration, and salt stress in transgenic tobacco.
Description: OSISAP1 overexpression in tobacco confers enhanced tolerance to cold, drought, and salt stress.

Synonyms: OsPYL3, OsRCAR1, REGULATORY COMPONENTS OF ABA RECEPTOR 3
Reference_title: Overexpression of ABA Receptor PYL10 Gene Confers Drought and Cold Tolerance to Indica Rice.
Description: Overexpression of rice ABA receptor gene PYL10 resulted in higher ABA levels in plants due to upregulated expression of genes for ABA biosynthesis including ZEP1, NCED1, NCED2, NCED3, and NCED4, enhancing yield formation of the drought-challenged plants through maintaining higher RWC, membrane stability index, chlorophyll content, and accumulated lower amount of MDA and H2O2 compared with WT plants

Reference_title: OsSDIR1 overexpression greatly improves drought tolerance in transgenic rice.
Description: The expression of OsSDIR1 was detected in all tissues of rice and up-regulated by drought and NaCl, but not by ABA. Upon drought treatment, the OsSDIR1-transgenic rice showed strong drought tolerance compared to control plants.

Gene name: Drought-, Heat-, and Salt-Induced RING Finger Protein 1
Reference_title: Oryza sativa drought-, heat-, and salt-induced RING finger protein 1 (OsDHSRP1) negatively regulates abiotic stress-responsive gene expression.
Description: The Arabidopsis plants overexpressing OsDHSRP1 exhibited hypersensitivity to drought, heat, and NaCl stress and a decrease in their germination rates and root lengths compared to the control plants. However, the OsDHSRP1-overexpressing plants showed no significant difference when treated with ABA. OsDHSRP1 E3 ligase acts as a negative regulator.

Synonyms: OsRING320, OsREIW1, RING-finger E3 ubiquitin ligases interacting with WRKY 1
Reference_title: Overexpression of OsRDCP1, a rice RING domain-containing E3 ubiquitin ligase, increased tolerance to drought stress in rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: OsRDCP1 was induced by drought stress. OsRDCP1-overexpressing T2 transgenic rice plants exhibited markedly improved tolerance to severe water deficits.

Reference_title: Rice A20/AN1 protein, OsSAP10, confers water-deficit stress tolerance via proteasome pathway and positive regulation of ABA signaling in Arabidopsis.
Description: Overexpression of OsRDCP1 increased tolerance to drought stress in transgenic rice plants. OsSAP10 transgenics showed improved tolerance to water-deficit stress at seed germination, seedling and mature plant stages. Heterologous expression of OsSAP10 in Arabidopsis enhances tolerance to water-deficit stress

Reference_title: A U-box E3 ubiquitin ligase OsPUB67 is positively involved in drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Over-expression of OsPUB67 improved drought stress tolerance by enhancing the reactive oxygen scavenging ability and stomatal closure.

Synonyms: NAC domain-containing protein 69, NAC domain-containing protein 87
Reference_title: OsNAC15 regulates drought and salt tolerance in rice.
Description: OsNAC15 positively regulates drought and salt tolerance and directly participates in the biosynthesis of abscisic acid (ABA).

Reference_title: Rice NAC transcription factor ONAC066 functions as a positive regulator of drought and oxidative stress response.
Description: Expression of ONAC066 was significantly induced by PEG, NaCl, H2O2 and abscisic acid (ABA). Overexpression of ONAC066 in transgenic rice improved drought and oxidative stress tolerance and increased ABA sensitivity, accompanied with decreased rate of water loss, increased contents of proline and soluble sugars, decreased accumulation of reactive oxygen species (ROS) and upregulated expression of stress-related genes under drought stress condition.

Synonyms: NAC6, NAC18, TMS 5, thermosensitive male sterility 5
Reference_title: Stress-induced nuclear translocation of ONAC023 improves drought and heat tolerance through multiple processes in rice.
Description: ONAC023 confers drought tolerance at vegetative and reproductive stages.

Reference_title: Overexpression of OsNAC14 Improves Drought Tolerance in Rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsNAC14 resulted in drought tolerance at the vegetative stage of growth. Field drought tests demonstrated that OsNAC14 overexpressing transgenic rice lines exhibited higher number of panicle and filling rate compared to non-transgenic plants under drought conditions. RNA-sequencing analysis revealed that OsNAC14 overexpression elevated the expression of genes for stress response, DNA damage repair, defense related, and strigolactone biosynthesis.

Reference_title: Over-expression of dehydrin gene, OsDhn1, improves drought and salt stress tolerance through scavenging of reactive oxygen species in rice (Oryza sativa L.).
Description: OsDhn1-overexpression transgenic rice plants (OXs) showed an enhanced drought and salt stress tolerance as judged by chlorophyll fluorescence (Fv/Fm), fresh and dry weight, water and chlorophyll content, and survival ratio.

Reference_title: A novel gene OsAHL1 improves both drought avoidance and drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Overexpression of OsAHL1 enhanced multiple stress tolerances in rice plants during both seedling and panicle development stages. Functional studies revealed that OsAHL1 regulates root development under drought condition to enhance drought avoidance, participates in oxidative stress response and also regulates the content of chlorophyll in rice leaves. Overexpression of OsAHL1 enhanced drought, salt and cold stress tolerance in rice seedling stage.

Reference_title: OsNAR2.1 Positively Regulates Drought Tolerance and Grain Yield Under Drought Stress Conditions in Rice.
Description: OsNAR2.1 is involved in drought stress tolerance through regulating drought stress sensitivity and stress-related gene expression in rice. OsNAR2.1 over-expression significantly increased grain yield as compared to WT and led to minimal growth defects in drought stress.

Reference_title: Rice Glycosyltransferase Gene UGT85E1 Is Involved in Drought Stress Tolerance Through Enhancing Abscisic Acid Response.
Description: The overexpression of UGT85E1 led to an enhanced tolerance in transgenic rice plants to drought stress, while the ugt85e1 mutants of rice showed a more sensitive phenotype to drought stress. UGT85E1 overexpression induced ABA accumulation, stomatal closure, enhanced reactive oxygen species (ROS) scavenging capacity, increased proline and sugar contents, and upregulated expression of stress-related genes under drought stress conditions.

Reference_title: Overexpressing heat-shock protein OsHSP50.2 improves drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsHSP50.2, an HSP90 family gene up-regulated by heat and osmotic stress treatments, positively regulates drought stress tolerance by modulating ROS homeostasis and osmotic adjustment in rice.

Reference_title: Overexpression of the OsERF71 Transcription Factor Alters Rice Root Structure and Drought Resistance.
Description: Overexpression of OsERF71, either throughout the entire plant or specifically in roots, resulted in a drought resistance phenotype at the vegetative growth stage, indicating that overexpression in roots was sufficient to confer drought resistance. OsERF71 alters root structure to enhance drought resistance.

Reference_title: Function of the HD-Zip I gene Oshox22 in ABA-mediated drought and salt tolerances in rice.
Description: Transgenic rice over-expressing Oshox22 showed increased sensitivity to ABA, increased ABA content, and decreased drought and salt tolerances.

Reference_title: Induced over-expression of the transcription factor OsDREB2A improves drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Transgenic lines over-expressing OsDREB2A were found to have improved survival rates under severe drought and salt stress conditions relative to non-transgenic rice plants or rice plants transformed with the empty vector control. OsDREB2A expression was found to be markedly induced by drought and ABA treatment.

Reference_title: OsCactin positively regulates the drought stress response in rice.
Description: OsCactin is regulated by OsTRAB1 and interacts with OsDi19 proteins to defend against drought stress. The OsCactin-overexpressing lines presented high tolerance to drought stress, whereas the OsCactin-knockout plants were sensitive to drought stress. OsTRAB1, a drought-responsive TF of the bZIP family, binds to the promoter of OsCactin as a drought-responsive regulator.

Reference_title: OsbZIP71, a bZIP transcription factor, confers salinity and drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsbZIP71 overexpressing rice lines significantly improved tolerance to drought, salt and PEG osmotic stresses. In contrast, RNAi knockdown transgenic lines were much more sensitive to salt, PEG osmotic stresses, and also ABA treatment.

Reference_title: Identification of rice Di19 family reveals OsDi19-4 involved in drought resistance.
Description: Overexpression of OsDi19 - 4 in rice increased drought tolerance by enhancing ROS-scavenging activity.

Reference_title: OsGRAS23, a rice GRAS transcription factor gene, is involved in drought stress response through regulating expression of stress-responsive genes.
Description: Overexpression of OsGRAS23 improved tolerance of transgenic rice plants to drought and oxidative stresses.

Synonyms: OsPL, Purple Leaf, transcription factor MYB8
Reference_title: Expression of OsMYB55 in maize activates stress-responsive genes and enhances heat and drought tolerance.
Description: Over-expression of OsMYB55 in maize decreased the negative effects of high temperature and drought resulting in improved plant growth and performance under these conditions. OsMYB55 transgenic maize shows tolerance to heat and drought stress.

Reference: Huda, Kazi M K et al. 2013
Reference_title: OsACA6, a P-type IIB Ca²⁺ ATPase promotes salinity and drought stress tolerance in tobacco by ROS scavenging and enhancing the expression of stress-responsive genes.
Description: OsACA6 transgenic plants had longer primary roots, and therefore are capable of producing more biomass and show improved drought tolerance under water-deficit conditions. Transgenic plants over-expressing OsACA6 efficiently modulate the ROS machinery and proline biosynthesis through an integrative mechanism.

Reference_title: Knockdown of OsPHP1 Leads to Improved Yield Under Salinity and Drought in Rice via Regulating the Complex Set of TCS Members and Cytokinin Signalling.
Description: OsPHP1 negatively influences abiotic stress tolerance, as evidenced by the increased sensitivity of OsPHP1-OE (overexpression) lines to salinity and drought.

Reference_title: Rice OsDof12 enhances tolerance to drought stress by activating the phenylpropanoid pathway.
Description: Overexpression of rice OsDof12 enhances tolerance to drought stress. Rice plants overexpressing OsDof12 (OsDof12-OE) displayed significantly higher tolerance to drought stress than the parental japonica rice “Dongjin”.

Reference_title: Unveiling the role of OsSAP17: Enhancing plant resistance to drought and salt.
Description: The ectopic expression of OsSAP17 significantly increased the capacity to withstand drought and salt stress in both transgenic yeast and Arabidopsis. Additionally, the ectopic expression of OsSAP17 led to notable changes in the expression of Arabidopsis ABA-related genes, including AtNCED3, AtABA2, and AtSnRK2.2.

Reference_title: OsLOX1 positively regulates seed vigor and drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Mutant lines with CRISPR/Cas9-induced knockout of OsLOX1 exhibited reduced tolerance to drought stress compared with the wild-type. This was accompanied by elevated levels of H2O2 and malondialdehyde, and a decrease in the expression levels of genes associated with antioxidant enzymes. OsLOX1 positively regulated rice seed vigor and drought stress.

Reference_title: OsbZIP27 coordinates with OsHUB1 and OsHUB2 to modulate drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsbZIP27 coordinates with OsHUB1/2 to enhance rice drought tolerance by increasing H2Bub and expression of OsHAK1 and OsGLN1. Loss of OsHUB1 and OsHUB2 function causes hypersensitivity to drought stress. OsHUB2 overexpression enhances drought tolerance.

Reference_title: Over-expression of XA21 binding protein 3 enhances rice survival under water-deficit stress.
Description: Over-expression of XB3 confers strong tolerance to water-deficit stress. Down-regulation of XB3 transcripts increases the sensitivity to water-deficit stress. XB3 interacts with OsDIS1 and can destabilize OsDIS1. OsDIS1 is a negative regulator of drought tolerance.

Reference_title: The SINA E3 ligase OsDIS1 negatively regulates drought response in rice
Description: Overexpression of OsDIS1 reduced drought tolerance in transgenic rice plants, while RNA interference silencing of OsDIS1 enhanced drought tolerance.

Reference_title: Gibberellin signalling mediates nucleocytoplasmic trafficking of Sucrose Synthase 1 to regulate the drought tolerance in rice.
Description: SLR1 negatively regulates the abundance of the dehydration-responsive protein OsBURP3 to enhance drought tolerance in rice. OsBURP3 facilitates the translocation of Sucrose Synthase 1 (OsSUS1), from the cytosol to the nucleus, thereby decreasing the sucrose content. Mutation of OsBURP3 reduces the nucleus accumulation of OsSUS1 to enhance drought tolerance.

Reference_title: Gibberellin signalling mediates nucleocytoplasmic trafficking of Sucrose Synthase 1 to regulate the drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsSUS1 positively regulates drought tolerance via mediating sucrose metabolism.

Reference_title: WOX11-OsPRX130 module confers rice drought tolerance by maintaining ROS homeostasis in rice root.
Description: WOX11 directly binds to the promoter of the peroxidase gene OsPRX130, thereby enhancing drought tolerance by regulating CR growth. Notably, OsPRX130 is predominantly expressed in rice roots and its expression is induced by drought stress. Knockout of OsPRX130 inhibited CR growth by reducing ROS levels, ultimately compromising the drought tolerance in rice.

Reference_title: WOX11-OsPRX130 module confers rice drought tolerance by maintaining ROS homeostasis in rice root.
Description: WOX11 directly binds to the promoter of the peroxidase gene OsPRX130, thereby enhancing drought tolerance by regulating CR growth. Notably, OsPRX130 is predominantly expressed in rice roots and its expression is induced by drought stress. Knockout of OsPRX130 inhibited CR growth by reducing ROS levels, ultimately compromising the drought tolerance in rice.

Synonyms: OsHAP3L SUBUNIT OF CCAAT-BOX BINDING COMPLEX
Reference_title: Overexpressing OsNF-YB12 elevated the content of jasmonic acid and impaired drought tolerance in rice.
Description: Rice overexpressing OsNF-YB12 was more sensitive to salinity and PEG osmotic stresses at seed germination stage, as well as reduced drought tolerance at seedling stage. Overexpressing OsNF-YB12 promoted JA biosynthesis and inhibit seed germination. Overexpression of OsNF-YB12 weakened drought tolerance in rice.

Synonyms: OsHAP2A SUBUNIT OF CCAAT-BOX BINDING COMPLEX
Reference_title: The NF-YA transcription factor OsNF-YA7 confers drought stress tolerance of rice in an abscisic acid independent manner
Description: Expression of OsNF-YA7 was induced by drought stress but not by ABA. Overexpression of OsNF-YA7 in transgenic rice plants improved drought tolerance. Consequently, OsNF-YA7 mediates rice drought tolerance in an ABA-independent manner.

Gene name: Clade A type 2 C protein phosphatase 68
Reference_title: The abscisic acid signaling negative regulator OsPP2C68 confers drought and salinity tolerance to rice.
Description: OsPP2C68 knockout mutants reduced seed germination and root and stem lengths under ABA treatment. OsPP2C68 knockout mutants exhibited altered synthesis of osmolytes and antioxidant enzymes under drought and high salinity stress, along with the differential expression of genes associated with drought and salt stress responses, enhancing rice tolerance to drought and salt.

Reference_title: OsSIDP366, a DUF1644 gene, positively regulates responses to drought and salt stresses in rice.
Description: Transgenic rice plants overexpressing OsSIDP366 showed enhanced drought and salinity tolerance and reduced water loss as compared to that in the control, whereas plants with downregulated OsSIDP366 expression levels using RNA interference (RNAi) were more sensitive to salinity and drought treatments.

Reference_title: Rice SUMO protease Overly Tolerant to Salt 1 targets the transcription factor, OsbZIP23 to promote drought tolerance in rice.
Description: OsOTS1 depleted transgenic plants accumulate more ABA and exhibit more productive agronomic traits during drought while OsOTS1 overexpressing lines are drought sensitive but ABA insensitive. OsOTS1 SUMO protease directly targets the ABA and drought responsive transcription factor OsbZIP23 for de-SUMOylation affecting its stability.

Reference_title: Heat shock factor OsHsfB2b negatively regulates drought and salt tolerance in rice.
Description: Expression of OsHsfB2b was strongly induced by heat, salt, ABA and PEG treatments. Drought and salt tolerances were significantly decreased by OsHsfB2b overexpression, but were enhanced by RNA interference.

Gene name: 16.9 kDa class I heat shock protein 2
Reference_title: Overexpression of OsHsp17.0 and OsHsp23.7 enhances drought and salt tolerance in rice.
Description: Transgenic rice overexpressing OsHsp17.0 and OsHsp23.7 significantly improve the tolerance to drought and salinity.
